timbre_sine: Pure sine — ONE frequency, no harmonics
quiz1_q: What happens to pitch when frequency increases?
quiz1_fb_goes_down: Backwards — MORE vibrations per second means HIGHER pitch. Slide frequency up with Play Sound on and listen to it climb.
quiz1_fb_stays_same: Pitch IS your ear’s perception of frequency — change one and you have changed the other.
quiz1_fb_disappears: Sound only disappears when AMPLITUDE reaches zero. Frequency changes the pitch, not whether it exists.
quiz2_fb_speed: Wave speed is set by the MEDIUM (air, water, string tension) — a louder shout does not arrive any sooner.
quiz2_fb_color: For light, FREQUENCY sets the color. Amplitude sets brightness — and for sound, loudness.
quiz2_fb_direction: Direction comes from the source and reflections. Amplitude is the wave’s height — how much energy it carries.
quiz3_fb_canceling: Canceling is only ONE special case (destructive). Superposition is the general rule: overlapping waves ADD their displacements — sometimes bigger, sometimes smaller.
quiz3_fb_reflecting: Reflection is a wave bouncing off a boundary. Superposition is what happens when two waves OVERLAP — try the Ripple Tank.
quiz3_fb_stopping: Waves pass straight through each other and continue unchanged — they never stop each other.
quiz5_q: Sound is what type of wave?
quiz6_q: At a node of a standing wave, the displacement is:
quiz7_q: The speed of a wave equals:
tour_step1: Welcome! Drag the Amplitude and Frequency sliders to shape your wave — or focus the wave itself and use the arrow keys. Space pauses.
tour_step2: Switch modes above: Standing, Ripple Tank, Reflection, Longitudinal, Doppler, and Spectrum each show a different piece of wave physics.
tour_step3: Turn on the Second Wave to see superposition — two waves adding into one. Try the 🎵 Beats preset, then Play Sound to hear it.
title: Wave Simulator
narrate_free: Free wave mode. Observe sine, square, triangle, and sawtooth waveforms.
narrate_standing: Standing wave mode. See nodes and antinodes form on a vibrating string.
narrate_ripple: Ripple tank mode. Drag two point sources to explore interference patterns.
narrate_reflection: Reflection mode. A wave hits a wall — drag the wall to see fixed vs free end behavior.
narrate_longitudinal: Longitudinal wave mode. See compression and rarefaction in a spring.
narrate_doppler: Doppler effect mode. A moving source shifts the observed frequency.
narrate_spectrum: Spectrum analysis mode. See the frequency components of your wave.
aria_pause_animation: Pause animation (or press Space on the wave)
aria_step_frame: Step the animation forward one frame
hero_free_title: Free Wave — sine, square, triangle, sawtooth
hero_free_hint: Pure sine = single frequency; square / triangle / sawtooth are sine sums (Fourier 1822). Each waveform sounds different at the same pitch — timbre IS the harmonic content.
hero_standing_title: Standing — nodes + antinodes on a string
hero_standing_hint: Two waves traveling opposite directions interfere into a stationary pattern. Guitar / violin strings vibrate at fundamental + integer harmonics. Length, tension, density set the pitch.
hero_ripple_hint: Where crests meet crests → bright (constructive); crests meet troughs → dark (destructive). Young’s 1801 double-slit experiment proved light is a wave; same physics here.
hero_longitudinal_hint: Sound is longitudinal: air molecules push and pull along the direction of travel, not perpendicular. P-waves in earthquakes are longitudinal too — they arrive first, hence the P (primary).
hero_doppler_hint: Source approaching = compressed wavelengths = higher pitch. Moving away = stretched wavelengths = lower pitch. Christian Doppler 1842; redshift in cosmology is the same idea, with light.
hero_spectrum_hint: Any periodic signal = sum of sines (Fourier transform). Music DAWs, JPEG, MRI, MP3 — all rest on this. Cooley + Tukey 1965 FFT made it computationally cheap; modern signal processing was born.
aria_canvas: Wave simulator — arrow up/down adjusts amplitude, arrow left/right adjusts frequency, +/- adjusts speed, space pauses or resumes the animation
overlay_jiggles: Jiggles in place — the wave travels, the matter does not
standing_nodes_prefix: Nodes (🔴 N): Points of zero displacement —
standing_antinodes_prefix: Antinodes (🟢 A): Points of maximum displacement —
ctrl_medium_aria: Medium wave speed in meters per second
vt_amplitude: — wave height; energy grows as amplitude squared
vt_medium: meters per second through the medium
toast_beats: Beats: 4 Hz + 4.5 Hz — watch (and hear) the slow 0.5 Hz pulse
aria_beats_preset: Beats preset — two waves at 4 and 4.5 hertz so the beat envelope is visible
aria_fixed_end: Fixed end (string tied down — phase inverts on reflection)
aria_free_end: Free end (string free to move — phase preserved on reflection)
aria_wall_position: Wall position across the tank (keyboard equivalent of dragging the gold wall)
refl_dashed_desc: : reflected alone · 💡 drag the gold wall or use the slider
aria_source_speed: Source speed as fraction of wave speed
doppler_tone_bends: the tone bends as the source passes the observer
doppler_press_play: press Play Sound to HEAR the shift
eq_standing_note: Standing wave — superposition of two traveling waves. A = Amplitude, n = Harmonic
investigate_intro: Ready to think like a physicist? Two open-ended investigations — no answer dumps, just you and the data.
toast_opened_below: Opened below — scroll down to the Reference Library
myth1_myth: Waves carry water (or air) along with them.
myth1_truth: Waves carry ENERGY, not matter. Each bit of the medium oscillates around its home spot and hands the motion on to its neighbor.
myth2_truth: Speed is set by the MEDIUM — tension, density, temperature. Cranking amplitude adds energy, not speed: the crests arrive no sooner.
myth3_myth: Sound can travel through empty space.
myth3_truth: Sound is matter compressing matter. In a vacuum there is nothing to push, so space is silent. Light is different — EM waves need no medium.
myth4_myth: When waves cancel, their energy is destroyed.
myth4_truth: Energy is never destroyed — it redistributes. The dark bands in the ripple tank sit right next to extra-bright ones; the total stays constant.
aria_reset_all: Reset all wave controls to their defaults
toast_sim_setup: Sim set up to demonstrate this — watch the wave
ai_unreachable: Could not reach AI tutor. Try again in a moment.
ai_placeholder: Click “Explain” for the AI tutor to describe the current wave at your chosen reading level.
wt_transverse_desc: Particle motion perpendicular to wave direction. Crests + troughs.
wt_longitudinal_desc: Particle motion parallel to wave direction. Compressions + rarefactions.
wt_surface_desc: Particles trace elliptical paths. Combines transverse + longitudinal.
wt_em_desc: Oscillating electric + magnetic fields. No medium needed (travel through vacuum).
wt_matter_desc: All matter has wave properties. λ = h/p. Observable for electrons + atoms.
wt_gravitational_desc: Ripples in spacetime from accelerating masses. First detected 2015 (LIGO).
wq_wavelength_def: Distance between two successive crests (or troughs). Crest-to-crest.
wq_frequency_def: Cycles per second. How many wave peaks pass a point per second.
wq_period_def: Time for one complete cycle. T = 1/f.
wq_amplitude_def: Maximum displacement from equilibrium. Carries energy; intensity ∝ A².
wq_wavespeed_def: How fast the wave moves through the medium. v = fλ.
wq_wavenumber_def: k = 2π/λ. Spatial frequency. Useful in y = A·sin(kx − ωt).
wq_angfreq_def: ω = 2πf. Used in trigonometric wave equations.
wq_phase_def: Position within a cycle. Two waves in phase: peaks align (constructive); out of phase by π: peaks meet troughs (destructive).
ip_constructive_result: Amplitudes ADD. Bright fringe (light), loud point (sound), large displacement.
ip_destructive_result: Amplitudes CANCEL. Dark fringe, quiet point, no displacement.
ip_partial_result: Partial reinforcement or cancellation. Wave appears with intermediate amplitude.
dc_toward_detail: Wavefronts bunch up in front. Higher pitch (sound) / blueshift (light).
dc_away_detail: Wavefronts spread out behind. Lower pitch / redshift.
dc_obs_toward_detail: You meet wavefronts faster. Higher apparent frequency.
dc_obs_away_detail: Wavefronts catch up to you slower.
dc_both_case: Both moving same direction, same speed
dc_both_detail: Relative velocity zero between source + observer.
dc_barrier_detail: Wavefronts stack on top of each other. Cone of compression follows the source.
em_ir_uses: Thermal imaging, TV remotes, fiber optics
em_gamma_uses: Cancer treatment, sterilization, astronomy (most energetic photons)
vc_blue_notes: Sky color (Rayleigh scattering favors short λ).
vc_orange_notes: Sunset enhances reds + oranges via scattering.
harm_fundamental_desc: Lowest mode. One antinode in the middle.
harm_2nd_desc: One full wavelength fits in the string length.
harm_3rd_desc: Three half-wavelengths fit. Common in plucked strings.
harm_4th_desc: Even integer; less common in stringed instruments tuned to fundamental.
harm_nth_desc: General formula. Higher harmonics give timbre / character to a note.
wf_intensity: Energy/area carried by wave grows as square of amplitude
wf_doppler: Doppler shift (general; signs depend on directions)
swi_guitar_note: Pluck position selects which harmonics dominate. Near center → odd harmonics.
swi_openpipe_note: Same formula as string. Wave equation symmetric.
swi_closedpipe_note: Only odd harmonics. Octave + 5th, not octave alone.
swi_drum_note: Inharmonic — doesn't sound like a clear pitch. Tympani tuned to approximate one.
swi_marimba_note: Undercut shapes the second partial to be a perfect octave above the fundamental.
si_traffic_notes: Hearing damage above this with extended exposure
si_max_notes: Pressure variation = atmospheric pressure (can't go louder without becoming shock wave)
gl_crest_def: Highest point of a transverse wave. Maximum positive displacement.
gl_trough_def: Lowest point of a transverse wave. Maximum negative displacement.
gl_compression_def: Region of high pressure in a longitudinal wave (sound).
gl_rarefaction_def: Region of low pressure in a longitudinal wave.
gl_node_def: Point of zero displacement on a standing wave.
gl_antinode_def: Point of maximum displacement on a standing wave.
gl_reflection_def: Wave bouncing off a boundary. Phase flips if going from less to more dense (hard boundary).
gl_refraction_def: Wave bending as it crosses a boundary where speed changes. Snell's law.
gl_diffraction_def: Wave bending around obstacles or through apertures. Effect strong when aperture ~ λ.
gl_interference_def: Two waves overlapping. Constructive (in phase) or destructive (out of phase).
gl_standingwave_def: Pattern from two identical waves traveling opposite directions. Nodes + antinodes fixed in space.
gl_resonance_def: Large amplitude when driving frequency matches natural frequency. Tacoma Narrows bridge collapse classic example (with caveats).
gl_damping_def: Loss of amplitude over time due to friction or radiation. Critical damping = fastest decay without overshoot.
gl_coherence_def: Constant phase relationship between waves. Required for stable interference patterns. Lasers are highly coherent.
gl_polarization_def: Direction of oscillation in a transverse wave. Light from sun is unpolarized; passing through a polarizer selects one direction.
gl_photon_def: Quantum of EM radiation. Energy E = hf. Each photon carries momentum p = h/λ.
exp_library_subtitle: Interactive references — pick a topic below to explore.
sec_types_intro: Waves are categorized by particle motion relative to propagation, and by whether they need a medium. All carry energy but no matter from one place to another.
sec_quantities_intro: The full vocabulary needed to describe any wave. Most quantities are related — once you know two, you can derive the rest.
sec_interference_intro: When two waves meet, their displacements add (principle of superposition). After passing through each other, each wave continues unchanged.
sec_beats_text: Two waves of slightly different frequency interfere alternately constructively + destructively. Beat frequency = |f₁ − f₂|. Musicians use this to tune by ear.
sec_doppler_intro: Frequency observed changes when source and/or observer move relative to each other. The ambulance siren that drops pitch as it passes you = sound Doppler. Galaxy redshift = light Doppler.
doppler_app_radar: Police radar: bounces microwaves off your car; Doppler shift = your speed.
doppler_app_ultrasound: Doppler ultrasound: measures blood flow direction + speed.
doppler_app_hubble: Hubble's discovery: distant galaxies are redshifted → universe expanding.
doppler_app_cmb: Cosmic microwave background: small Doppler shifts reveal early-universe structure.
sec_spectrum_intro: All EM waves travel at c in vacuum. The full spectrum spans 18+ orders of magnitude in frequency. We see only a narrow sliver (visible light). Energy per photon: E = hf — higher frequency = more energetic.
sec_colors_intro: Human vision spans ~380-700 nm. Each color corresponds to a specific range of wavelengths. White light = mix of all visible wavelengths.
sec_harmonics_intro: A vibrating string supports an infinite series of modes called harmonics. Each has a specific number of nodes (zero-amplitude points) + antinodes (max-amplitude points). The mix of harmonics = timbre.
harm_callout_text: A note at 440 Hz on a violin and a flute both have fundamental at 440 Hz. The MIX of higher harmonics (the spectrum) is different — that's timbre. Pure sine = boring; rich harmonic content = recognizable instrument.
sec_instruments_intro: Instruments make tones by setting up standing waves. The boundary conditions (open/closed/fixed ends) determine which harmonics are allowed.
sec_decibels_intro: Decibels are a logarithmic scale. +10 dB ≈ 10× intensity, perceived as ~2× as loud. dB SPL (sound pressure level) is referenced to threshold of human hearing.
db_callout_text: OSHA: 85 dB for 8 hours, 90 dB for 4 hours, etc. (each +5 dB halves safe exposure time). Wear ear protection at concerts, with power tools, at firing ranges.
sec_discover_intro: A string is fixed at both ends. Wiggle the controls. Watch what changes and what stays the same. Log a few observations, then try to spot the pattern. Hit "I see it" when you think you have the law — no peeking.
disc_mu_note: (string mass density μ fixed at 0.01 kg/m)
disc_hypothesis_text: Compare your f × λ column. Is it constant for different f values at the same tension? What about when you change tension? Form a hypothesis before clicking "I see it".
disc_reveal_text: For a wave on a string, the speed v depends ONLY on the string's tension and mass density (v = √(T/μ)). The product f·λ always equals v. Change frequency → wavelength compensates so f·λ stays constant. Increase tension → wave speed jumps → f·λ jumps with it. Frequency × wavelength is one of the deepest constancies in physics: it shows up identically for light, sound, water waves, and quantum matter waves.
disc_reveal_hertz: You just discovered it the way Hertz did — by watching what stays constant when you change one variable at a time.
sh_hypothesis_label: Your hypothesis (free text — no right answer):
sh_hypothesis_placeholder: What pattern do you notice in the values where the string locks into a clean standing wave? Type your own theory.
sh_stuck_btn: I'm stuck — show me some questions to think about (no answers)
sh_open_questions: Open questions (no answers — investigate by manipulating):
sh_q1: Try f = 5 Hz at T = 100 N, then T = 25 N. Does the locked state happen at the same f? Why might that be?
sh_q2: Sweep frequency slowly from 1 → 20 Hz at fixed T. How many locked states do you find? Are they evenly spaced or pattern-spaced?
sh_q3: When you triple the tension, does the f of the first locked state triple, or change by some other factor? Predict before you check.
sh_q4: Log 4-5 locked observations. Look at the n column — what value do they share? Is that a coincidence or a constraint?
sh_q5: What relationship between f, T, and L would have to be true for an integer number of half-wavelengths to fit on the string?
sh_no_answers: No answers will be revealed here. The point is to push your thinking, not to hand you a result.
sh_understood_label: I think I understand the pattern now — let me explain it in my own words
sh_explanation_placeholder: Explain in your own words: what determines when a standing wave forms? What role does tension play? What role does frequency play? What is "n"?
sh_saved_note: Saved. Notice — nobody checked your answer. The point of inquiry is the thinking that produced it, not external validation.
sh_design_note: Design note: this widget has no score, no chips, no reveal button, and no right-answer check. Everything you discover comes from manipulation and observation. That is what "inquiry" looks like when the answer-dump is removed.
oel1_behavior: Bends parallel rays toward focal point
oel1_use: Magnifying glass, eyeglasses for farsightedness, camera lenses, microscope objectives.
oel2_behavior: Spreads parallel rays as if from focal point
oel3_behavior: Reflects parallel rays through focal point
oel3_use: Reflecting telescopes, satellite dishes, makeup mirrors (magnification).
oel4_behavior: Reflects parallel rays as if from focal point
oel4_use: Car side mirrors ("objects in mirror are closer than they appear"), store security mirrors.
oel5_behavior: Flat reflection — image distance = object distance
oel5_use: Everyday mirrors. Image is virtual, upright, same-size, laterally inverted.
oel6_behavior: Separates white light into spectrum by refraction
oel6_use: Spectroscopy, rainbow demos. Different wavelengths refract by different amounts (dispersion).
oel7_behavior: Separates light by many parallel slits
oel7_use: Higher resolution than prism. Used in modern spectrometers.
oel8_behavior: Transmits part of beam, reflects rest
oel9_use: Sunglasses (cut glare from water/road), LCD displays, photography filters.
oel10_behavior: Total internal reflection guides light along the core
oel10_use: Internet backbone, endoscopes, fiber-optic Christmas trees.
oel11_use: One-way windows (only works with brightness asymmetry), Michelson interferometer.
oel12_behavior: Returns light to source regardless of angle
oel12_use: Bike reflectors, road signs, Apollo lunar laser ranging.
ri_vacuum_notes: Reference. Light travels at c = 299,792,458 m/s.
ri_air_notes: Essentially same as vacuum for most purposes.
ri_glycerin_notes: Submerged glass nearly invisible in glycerin (similar n).
ri_flint_notes: Higher dispersion than crown — used in achromatic doublets.
ri_cz_notes: Higher than glass — used as diamond simulant.
ri_diamond_notes: Very high. Critical angle just 24.4° → "fire" via total internal reflection.
ri_silicon_notes: Opaque to visible but transparent in IR. Used in IR optics.
ri_meta_notes: Engineered structures bend light backward. Cloaking research.
df_singleslit_notes: a = slit width. Central bright fringe is twice as wide as side fringes.
df_doubleslit_notes: d = slit separation. Demonstrates wave nature of light (Young, 1801).
df_grating_notes: Many slits, very sharp peaks. Resolution improves with more slits.
df_rayleigh_notes: Smallest angle resolvable by circular aperture of diameter D. Limits telescope/microscope resolution.
df_bragg_notes: X-rays diffract from crystal planes. Used to determine atomic positions.
df_spotsize_notes: NA = numerical aperture. Visible light limit ~200 nm — why electron microscopes have higher resolution.
pol_whatis_detail: Direction of oscillation of the electric field in EM waves. Sound (longitudinal) cannot be polarized.
pol_linear_detail: E-field oscillates in single plane. Most "polarized" light is linear.
pol_circular_detail: E-field rotates in circle. Right vs left circular. Used in 3D movies.
pol_malus_detail: I = I₀·cos²θ. Light through two polarizers: angle between them controls intensity.
pol_brewster_detail: tan θ_B = n₂/n₁. Reflected light is fully polarized at this angle of incidence.
pol_birefringence_detail: Some crystals (calcite, quartz) split unpolarized light into two polarized rays.
pol_glare_detail: Reflection off horizontal surfaces (water, road) polarizes horizontally. Polarized sunglasses block this.
pol_lcds_detail: Two crossed polarizers + liquid crystals → controllable transmission per pixel.
pol_stress_detail: Polarized light through plastic models shows stress patterns as color bands.
pol_3d_detail: Circular polarization (RealD) or linear at 45°/135° (older systems) separates left/right images.
qw_debroglie_detail: λ = h/p. Every massive particle has a wave nature. For an electron at 100 eV, λ ≈ 0.12 nm.
qw_duality_detail: Photons + electrons show both wave + particle behavior. Single-photon double-slit experiments confirm interference even with one photon at a time.
qw_schrodinger_detail: Wave equation for matter waves. Solutions ψ give probability amplitude. |ψ|² = probability density.
qw_heisenberg_detail: Δx · Δp ≥ ℏ/2. Position + momentum can't both be measured precisely. Fundamental, not measurement error.
qw_quantized_detail: Bound systems (atoms, harmonic oscillators) only allow specific energy levels. Like standing waves on a string.
qw_tunneling_detail: Wave nature lets particles "tunnel" through energy barriers classically forbidden. Basis of STM, alpha decay, flash memory.
qw_photon_detail: E = hf = hc/λ. Higher frequency → higher-energy photon. UV photons can ionize; IR cannot.
qw_compton_detail: Photon-electron collision shifts photon wavelength. Confirms photons carry momentum p = h/λ.
qw_pauli_detail: Identical fermions cannot occupy same quantum state. Why electrons fill shells; why matter has structure.
qw_coherence_detail: Distance over which wave maintains phase. Laser: kilometers. Sunlight: micrometers. Needed for interference.
sw_p_notes: Fastest. Compression/rarefaction along travel direction. Travels through solids AND liquids — passes through Earth's outer core.
sw_s_notes: Slower than P. Side-to-side shaking. Cannot travel through liquids → outer core blocks S-waves (key evidence it's liquid).
sw_love_notes: Horizontal transverse motion at surface. More damaging to buildings than P/S.
sw_rayleigh_notes: Rolling motion (like ocean waves). Largest amplitude. Felt as the rolling shake of an earthquake.
sf_richter_detail: Logarithmic. Each +1 magnitude = ~10× amplitude, ~32× energy. M7 releases ~1000× energy of M5.
sf_moment_detail: Used for modern measurements, especially large quakes. Replaced Richter for M > ~6.5.
sf_mercalli_detail: Felt-effects scale (I-XII). What people experienced, not energy released. Same quake = different intensity at different distances.
sf_epicenter_detail: 3 stations measure P-S arrival-time difference → distance to each → triangulate epicenter.
sf_structure_detail: P/S wave behavior revealed liquid outer core, solid inner core, mantle, crust. Discontinuities mapped.
sf_tsunami_detail: Underwater quake → ocean displacement → long-wavelength wave traveling ~700 km/h in deep water. P-wave arrival gives ~minutes warning before tsunami.
sf_largest_detail: Chile 1960: M9.5 (~32× energy of biggest US quake — 1964 Alaska M9.2). Tsunami crossed Pacific.
ow_wind_notes: Local wind generated. Variable height + direction.
ow_swell_notes: Wind waves that have traveled far. Sorted into regular sets.
ow_tsunami_notes: Tiny height in deep ocean (<1 m) but VERY long wavelength. Pile up at shore.
ow_tide_notes: Gravitational pull of Moon + Sun. Bulges in ocean rotate as Earth turns.
ow_seiche_notes: Standing wave in enclosed/semi-enclosed water (lakes, harbors). Triggered by wind, pressure, quakes.
ow_rogue_notes: Unusually large (>2× average). Once thought myth, now confirmed; constructive interference of swells.
of_deep_detail: Depth > λ/2. Wave speed depends only on λ: v = √(gλ/2π).
of_shallow_detail: Depth < λ/20. Wave speed depends only on depth: v = √(gd). Tsunamis are always shallow-water waves (huge λ).
of_breaking_detail: Waves break when amplitude/wavelength > ~1/7. Crest moves faster than trough → tips over.
of_sigheight_detail: Average height of tallest 1/3 of waves — better matches what a sailor would call "the wave height".
of_tides_detail: Spring (extreme): Sun + Moon aligned (new/full moon). Neap (mild): Sun + Moon at 90° (quarter moons).
ant_dipole_use: Simplest resonant antenna. FM radio dipole is ~1.5 m.
ant_yagi_use: Directional. Old rooftop TV antennas, ham radio.
ant_dish_use: Satellite TV, radio astronomy, deep-space comm.
ant_patch_use: GPS, Wi-Fi, phones. Flat, easy to mass-manufacture.
ant_phased_use: Steer beam electronically. Modern radar, 5G mmWave, Starlink user terminals.
rs_primary_use: Aircraft detection. Pulse sent → echo measured. Range = c·Δt/2.
rs_doppler_use: Weather (rain motion → wind), police speed guns. Frequency shift → velocity.
rs_sar_use: Moving platform creates effective large aperture. Earth imaging from satellites; works through clouds + at night.
rs_phased_use: Steerable beam without moving the dish. AEGIS, modern fighters.
rs_active_use: Send pulse, listen for echo. Submarine detection, fish-finding. Limited range due to sound absorption.
rs_passive_use: Listen for sounds from targets. Submarines use this when avoiding detection (no emissions).
rs_echo_use: Bats, dolphins. Frequencies up to ~200 kHz (bats); chirps adapted for prey.
rs_ultrasound_use: 1–20 MHz pulses image internal tissues. Doppler mode shows blood flow.
rs_lidar_use: Laser pulses → 3D point clouds. Self-driving cars, archaeology (forest-floor mapping).
shk_mach_detail: M = v/v_sound. M<1 subsonic, M=1 transonic, M>1 supersonic, M>5 hypersonic.
shk_boom_detail: Cone of compressed air trailing supersonic objects. Heard as boom when cone passes you.
shk_cone_detail: sin α = 1/M. Faster object → narrower cone.
shk_thickness_detail: Just a few mean free paths (~micrometers in atmosphere). Steep pressure jump.
shk_speed_detail: 343 m/s = 1235 km/h = 767 mph. Increases with temperature: v ≈ 331 + 0.6·T(°C) m/s.
shk_media_detail: Water: ~1480 m/s. Steel: ~5000 m/s. Hotter, denser, stiffer → faster sound.
shk_whip_detail: Tip of whip exceeds Mach 1 → mini sonic boom.
shk_explosion_detail: Initial pressure jump (overpressure) travels faster than sound, slowing to sound speed with distance.
hist_1690: Wave theory of light. Huygens' principle (every point on wavefront is source of secondary wavelets).
hist_1801: Double-slit experiment. Established wave nature of light through interference.
hist_1818: Mathematical theory of diffraction. Predicted (correctly) the "Poisson spot" — bright dot in center of circular shadow.
hist_1842: Doppler effect for sound and light.
hist_1864: Maxwell's equations — light is electromagnetic wave. Predicted speed = c.
hist_1887: Produced + detected radio waves in lab. Confirmed Maxwell's prediction.
hist_1900: E = hf — energy is quantized. Birth of quantum theory.
hist_1905: Photoelectric effect — light comes in quanta (photons). Nobel 1921.
hist_1924: Matter waves: λ = h/p. Even electrons are waves.
hist_1927: Confirmed electron diffraction — proved matter waves.
hist_2015: First direct detection of gravitational waves (binary black hole merger).
sec_refraction_intro: Snell's law: n₁ sin θ₁ = n₂ sin θ₂. Light slows by factor n in material; n is wavelength-dependent (dispersion).
sec_diffraction_intro: Diffraction = bending of waves around obstacles or through openings. Most noticeable when feature size ≈ wavelength.
sec_quantum_intro: In quantum mechanics, every particle has wave properties. The "wave function" ψ gives probability amplitude.
sec_antennas_intro: Antenna size typically tied to wavelength. Lower frequency = longer wavelength = bigger antenna.
